Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class SystemStatusViewModel

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

View model for displaying Apple IIe system status including soft switches and pushbutton states.

public sealed class SystemStatusViewModel : ReactiveObject, IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>, IHandleObservableErrors, IReactiveObject, INotifyPropertyChanged, INotifyPropertyChanging, IEnableLogger
Inheritance
object
ReactiveObject
SystemStatusViewModel
Implements
IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>

IReactiveObject

INotifyPropertyChanging

ReactiveObject.PropertyChanged

object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides reactive properties for all Apple IIe soft switches, pushbuttons, and system state flags. These properties are bound to UI elements that display the current hardware configuration and status.

Update Pattern: Unlike EmulatorStateViewModel, this view model uses immediate subscription (no WhenActivated) because system status updates are relatively infrequent and always needed for accurate display. Updates are pushed from ISystemStatusProvider.Stream.

Initialization: The constructor immediately subscribes to the status stream and applies the current snapshot to initialize all properties with correct initial values.

Property Count: Contains 24 boolean properties representing:

  • 6 memory mapping switches (80STORE, RAMRD, RAMWRT, INTCXROM, ALTZP, SLOTC3ROM)
  • 3 pushbuttons (PB0, PB1, PB2)
  • 4 annunciators (AN0, AN1, AN2, AN3/DGR)
  • 6 video mode switches (PAGE2, HIRES, MIXED, TEXT, 80COL, ALTCHAR)
  • 1 cursor flash state (FLASHON)
  • 4 language card banking switches (BANK1, PREWRITE, HIGHREAD, HIGHWRITE)

Constructors

SystemStatusViewModel(ISystemStatusProvider)

Initializes a new instance of the SystemStatusViewModel class.

public SystemStatusViewModel(ISystemStatusProvider status)

Parameters

status ISystemStatusProvider

System status provider supplying reactive status updates.

Remarks

Immediate Subscription: Unlike EmulatorStateViewModel, this view model subscribes immediately to the status stream without using WhenActivated. This ensures system status is always up-to-date and available for display.

Initialization: After subscribing to the stream, applies the current snapshot from Current to initialize all properties with correct starting values. This prevents a brief period where properties would be default (false) until the first update arrives.

Update Frequency: System status updates are relatively infrequent (typically triggered by soft switch changes or periodic polling), so immediate subscription has minimal performance impact compared to the frequently-updating EmulatorState.

Properties

State80Store

Gets the state of the 80STORE soft switch.

public bool State80Store { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when 80STORE is active, false otherwise.

Remarks

When active, the PAGE2 switch controls auxiliary memory access rather than display page selection. Affects memory mapping for $0400-$07FF and $2000-$3FFF ranges.

StateAltCharSet

Gets the state of the ALTCHAR soft switch.

public bool StateAltCharSet { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when using alternate character set, false for primary character set.

Remarks

Selects between primary and alternate character sets in text mode. The alternate character set includes MouseText characters and inverse lowercase letters.

StateAltZp

Gets the state of the ALTZP soft switch.

public bool StateAltZp { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when using auxiliary zero page/stack, false when using main memory.

Remarks

When active, zero page ($0000-$00FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) are mapped to auxiliary RAM. Allows programs to maintain separate zero page/stack contexts.

StateAnn0

Gets the state of annunciator 0.

public bool StateAnn0 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when annunciator 0 is on, false when off.

Remarks

General-purpose output bit controlled by $C058/$C059. Often used for peripheral control or as a flag for custom hardware.

StateAnn1

Gets the state of annunciator 1.

public bool StateAnn1 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when annunciator 1 is on, false when off.

Remarks

General-purpose output bit controlled by $C05A/$C05B. Often used for peripheral control or as a flag for custom hardware.

StateAnn2

Gets the state of annunciator 2.

public bool StateAnn2 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when annunciator 2 is on, false when off.

Remarks

General-purpose output bit controlled by $C05C/$C05D. Often used for peripheral control or as a flag for custom hardware.

StateAnn3

Gets the state of annunciator 3 (also known as DGR - Double Graphics).

public bool StateAnn3 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when annunciator 3 is on, false when off.

Remarks

General-purpose output bit controlled by $C05E/$C05F. Commonly used to enable double hi-res graphics mode on the Apple IIe.

Double Hi-Res Mode: When combined with other switches, enables 560x192 16-color graphics mode using both main and auxiliary memory.

StateBank1

Gets the state of the BANK1 soft switch.

public bool StateBank1 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when using language card bank 1, false when using bank 2.

Remarks

Controls which 4KB bank of RAM is mapped into $D000-$DFFF. Bank 2 is at lower addresses, bank 1 is at higher addresses in the physical RAM chip.

StateFlashOn

Gets the cursor/mode indicator flash state.

public bool StateFlashOn { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when flash is on (visible), false when flash is off (hidden).

Remarks

Toggles at approximately 2.1 Hz (~476ms period) to control cursor blinking and mode indicator flashing. Used for inverse/flashing characters in text mode.

Usage: Characters with bit 6 set (flashing) alternate between normal and inverse video based on this state.

StateHiRes

Gets the state of the HIRES soft switch.

public bool StateHiRes { get; }

Property Value

bool

True for hi-res graphics mode, false for low-res graphics mode.

Remarks

Controls whether graphics mode uses hi-res (280x192 or 560x192) or low-res (40x48). Only affects graphics mode; has no effect when TEXT mode is active.

StateHighRead

Gets the HIGHREAD soft switch state.

public bool StateHighRead { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when reading from language card RAM, false when reading from ROM.

Remarks

Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF come from RAM or ROM. Independent of write enable.

StateHighWrite

Gets the HIGHWRITE soft switch state.

public bool StateHighWrite { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when writing to language card RAM is enabled, false when write-protected.

Remarks

Controls whether writes to $D000-$FFFF are allowed to modify RAM. Requires the two-access write enable sequence (PREWRITE) to activate.

Write Enable Sequence: Two consecutive accesses to specific language card addresses are required to enable writing, preventing accidental ROM overwrites.

StateIntCxRom

Gets the state of the INTCXROM soft switch.

public bool StateIntCxRom { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when using internal ROM, false when using peripheral card ROMs.

Remarks

Controls whether accesses to $C100-$CFFF use internal ROM or peripheral card ROMs. When true (default at power-on), internal ROM is active; when false, slot ROMs are accessible.

StateMixed

Gets the state of the MIXED soft switch.

public bool StateMixed { get; }

Property Value

bool

True for mixed text/graphics mode, false for full-screen mode.

Remarks

When active, displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom (lines 20-23). Commonly used for games to show status information while displaying graphics.

StatePage2

Gets the state of the PAGE2 soft switch.

public bool StatePage2 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when displaying page 2, false when displaying page 1.

Remarks

Controls which video page is displayed (page 1 at $0400 or page 2 at $0800). Behavior is affected by 80STORE switch.

When 80STORE is off: PAGE2 selects display page. When 80STORE is on: PAGE2 affects auxiliary memory access instead.

StatePb0

Gets the state of pushbutton 0 (game controller button 0).

public bool StatePb0 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when button 0 is pressed, false when released.

Remarks

Readable at $C061. Typically used for joystick or paddle button 0.

StatePb1

Gets the state of pushbutton 1 (game controller button 1).

public bool StatePb1 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when button 1 is pressed, false when released.

Remarks

Readable at $C062. Typically used for joystick or paddle button 1.

StatePb2

Gets the state of pushbutton 2 (game controller button 2).

public bool StatePb2 { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when button 2 is pressed, false when released.

Remarks

Readable at $C063. Typically used for additional game controller buttons.

StatePrewrite

Gets the PREWRITE state (first step of write enable sequence).

public bool StatePrewrite { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when first access has occurred, false otherwise.

Remarks

First step in the two-access write enable sequence. When true, the next access to the appropriate language card address will enable writing (set HIGHWRITE).

Implementation Note: This is an internal state flag, not directly controlled by a specific soft switch address. It's set by the first of two required accesses to enable language card RAM writing.

StateRamRd

Gets the state of the RAMRD soft switch.

public bool StateRamRd { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when reading from auxiliary RAM, false when reading from main RAM.

Remarks

Controls whether reads from certain memory ranges ($0200-$BFFF) come from main or auxiliary RAM. Typically used with 80-column card or extended memory applications.

StateRamWrt

Gets the state of the RAMWRT soft switch.

public bool StateRamWrt { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when writing to auxiliary RAM, false when writing to main RAM.

Remarks

Controls whether writes to certain memory ranges ($0200-$BFFF) go to main or auxiliary RAM. Can be different from RAMRD, allowing read-from-one/write-to-other configurations.

StateShow80Col

Gets the state of the 80COL (80VID) soft switch.

public bool StateShow80Col { get; }

Property Value

bool

True for 80-column display, false for 40-column display.

Remarks

When active, enables 80-column text mode using both main and auxiliary video memory. Requires auxiliary memory (80-column card or equivalent).

StateSlotC3Rom

Gets the state of the SLOTC3ROM soft switch.

public bool StateSlotC3Rom { get; }

Property Value

bool

True when using slot 3 ROM, false when using internal ROM.

Remarks

Controls whether accesses to $C300-$C3FF use internal ROM or slot 3 peripheral ROM. Independent of INTCXROM, allowing slot 3 to be controlled separately.

StateTextMode

Gets the state of the TEXT soft switch.

public bool StateTextMode { get; }

Property Value

bool

True for text mode, false for graphics mode.

Remarks

Master switch controlling text vs graphics display. When true, displays 40-column or 80-column text. When false, displays graphics (low-res or hi-res based on HIRES switch).
